author: @getsmartr
text:
What about using tapered surfaces to increase roller contact and pressure without losing simplicity.  

Each roller has a cone on the end

The end piece has a free floating support cone in the center and a fixed tapered cup on the perimeter.  Preload on the cones could be adjusted using pusher bolts and clamping screws.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I like that mate - clever idea thanks for sharing

author: @rjung_ch

author: @martylawson1638
text: You have a "free" clyndrical diameter still, and gears can be made to any pitch.  So you can still get really high ratios with one or two gear stages in the drive.  What you are loosing with gears is smoothness of motion.  It's VERY had to make gears smooth enough to keep the output moving in the correct direction with 1000:1 or higher ratios achieved via subtraction.

author: @azlandpilotcar4450
text: Very good.  It is still an excellent winch design.  Can it be back driven?  Electric motors require high gear ratio reductions in order to be efficient for e.g. automobiles.  If this is cheaper and lighter than comparable gearing, it would be a great engineering compromise.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate and yes it can be backdriven
